deep_dive.title: Hallucination Self-Play: Bootstrapping Reinforced Detector via Evolved Generator
deep_dive.abstract: Identifying faithfulness hallucinations in LLM-generated outputs remains challenging due to the scarcity of high-quality annotated data. Recent work relies on advanced LLMs to synthesize training data, including rationales, labels, and hallucinated claims. However, these methods treat the generator as a static component, limiting iterative improvement of the detector. To address this limitation, we introduce Hallucination Self-Play (HSP), a novel framework that enables the detector to bootstrap with an evolved generator. HSP involves two roles initialized from the same base model, a detector that assesses the faithfulness of model outputs, and a generator that produces increasingly hard-to-detect hallucinated responses. Specifically, the detector is first fine-tuned on human-labeled data and then employed as a reward model to train the generator via reinforcement learning from AI feedback (RLAIF). In turn, the evolved generator synthesizes hallucination data to further optimize the detector through rule-based reinforcement learning. Experiments on RAGTruth benchmark and two model families demonstrate that the proposed framework can progressively enhance a small LLM to match or even outperform advanced LLMs without external supervision. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Hallucination-Self-Play-50B5 .
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过自博弈识别忠实性幻觉
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过自博弈识别忠实性幻觉
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过自博弈识别忠实性幻觉
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Hallucination Self-Play (HSP), a framework where a detector and a generator initialized from the same base model co-evolve, enabling the detector to identify increasingly hard-to-detect faithfulness hallucinations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有幻觉检测方法依赖静态生成器合成数据，限制了检测器的迭代提升。本文提出幻觉自博弈框架，使检测器与不断进化的生成器进行对抗博弈，生成器产生更难检测的幻觉，从而引导检测器持续增强识别忠实性幻觉的能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有幻觉检测方法依赖静态生成器合成数据，限制了检测器的迭代提升。本文提出幻觉自博弈框架，使检测器与不断进化的生成器进行对抗博弈，生成器产生更难检测的幻觉，从而引导检测器持续增强识别忠实性幻觉的能力。

deep_dive.title: Game Theory Driven Multi-Agent Framework Mitigates Language Model Hallucination
deep_dive.abstract: The application of lightweight Large Language Models in rule-based scientific domains remains severely limited by their tendency to mimic linguistic patterns rather than reproduce axiomatic reasoning, causing frequent hallucinations. Here, we show that G-Frame, an adaptive multi-agent framework integrating Bayesian and team game principles, establishes an automated closed-loop for high-quality data synthesis and model training. By forcing the internalization of domain constraints through structured reasoning, we synthesized a specialized corpus of 363,045 chains-of-thought and 199,589 question-answer pairs. The resulting 7B model OmniChem achieves performance parity with GPT 4o mini on custom benchmarks and ChemBench while exhibiting a 79.46% reduction in hallucinations relative to its base architecture. We further demonstrate the advanced capabilities of OmniChem in molecular design and synthesis planning. This work establishes a scalable paradigm utilizing adaptive multi-agents to overcome inherent reasoning deficiencies, offering a feasible pathway for accelerating knowledge discovery in specialized scientific fields.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Mitigating language model hallucination via multi-agent framework
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过多智能体框架缓解语言模型幻觉
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过多智能体框架缓解语言模型幻觉
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过多智能体框架缓解语言模型幻觉
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: G-Frame mitigates hallucinations in lightweight LLMs for rule-based scientific domains by using a game theory driven multi-agent framework to synthesize high-quality data and enforce domain constraints.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 轻量级大语言模型在基于规则的科学领域中常因模仿语言模式而非公理推理产生幻觉。本文提出G-Frame，一种结合贝叶斯和团队博弈原则的自适应多智能体框架，通过强制内化领域约束来合成高质量数据并训练模型。实验表明，该方法训练的7B模型在化学基准上达到GPT-4o mini的性能，且幻觉降低了79.46%，为缓解大模型幻觉提供了有效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 轻量级大语言模型在基于规则的科学领域中常因模仿语言模式而非公理推理产生幻觉。本文提出G-Frame，一种结合贝叶斯和团队博弈原则的自适应多智能体框架，通过强制内化领域约束来合成高质量数据并训练模型。实验表明，该方法训练的7B模型在化学基准上达到GPT-4o mini的性能，且幻觉降低了79.46%，为缓解大模型幻觉提供了有效方案。

deep_dive.title: Can We Trust LLM's Logic? Quantifying Uncertainty, Coherence, and Robustness via a Graph-Based Framework
deep_dive.abstract: Large-Language Models (LLMs) can be prone to flawed and unfaithful reasoning that decoding strategies like Self-Consistency (SC) fail to detect as they evaluate only final-answer agreement while ignoring the logical validity of intermediate steps. This raises three fundamental questions: How can we reliably quantify uncertainty in LLM reasoning? Can semantic, structural, and causal awareness select more faithful reasoning compared to naïve majority voting? and How robust is reasoning topology under adversarial conditions? To address these questions, we introduce GRAPHEVAL, a graph-based reasoning framework that re-frames uncertainty quantification (UQ) as a holistic reasoning fidelity problem. We propose a novel UQ metric, Graph Reasoning Coherence Score (GRCS), that quantifies semantic-structural consensus of the reasoning space and captures pathological mode collapse and confident hallucinations. We find that GRCS is the only metric that is consistently negatively correlated with reasoning faithfulness across both more capable and smaller models. Additionally, we introduce Graph Self-Consistency (GSC), a medoid-based decoding strategy that trades nominal accuracy for reasoning fidelity, exposing the degree to which SC is inflated by unfaithful lucky guesses in smaller models, while preserving or improving accuracy in more capable ones. Finally, through adversarial medoid ablation, we demonstrate that the GSC-selected path acts as a "load-bearing path" and forcing models away from it degrades reasoning faithfulness and, in targeted cases, causes drops in accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Detecting unfaithful reasoning and quantifying uncertainty
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 检测不忠实推理和量化不确定性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 检测不忠实推理和量化不确定性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 检测不忠实推理和量化不确定性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LLMs often produce unfaithful reasoning that standard decoding misses. GRAPHEVAL is a graph-based framework that quantifies uncertainty and detects flawed reasoning by evaluating semantic, structural, and causal validity, providing a robust method for detecting hallucinations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型容易产生有缺陷和不忠实的推理，而现有的自一致性等解码策略仅评估最终答案的一致性，忽略了中间步骤的逻辑有效性。本文提出GRAPHEVAL框架，将不确定性量化重新构建为推理保真度问题，利用基于图的方法评估语义、结构和因果感知以选择更忠实的推理。该框架能够可靠地量化LLM推理中的不确定性，并在对抗条件下检测出不可靠的推理拓扑，通过图结构评估推理的逻辑有效性可以有效检测大语言模型中的不忠实推理和幻觉。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大语言模型容易产生有缺陷和不忠实的推理，而现有的自一致性等解码策略仅评估最终答案的一致性，忽略了中间步骤的逻辑有效性。本文提出GRAPHEVAL框架，将不确定性量化重新构建为推理保真度问题，利用基于图的方法评估语义、结构和因果感知以选择更忠实的推理。该框架能够可靠地量化LLM推理中的不确定性，并在对抗条件下检测出不可靠的推理拓扑，通过图结构评估推理的逻辑有效性可以有效检测大语言模型中的不忠实推理和幻觉。

quick_skim.title: Structured Schemas for Provenance-Rich, LLM-Assisted QSP Model Calibration
quick_skim.abstract: Quantitative systems pharmacology (QSP) models require calibration data from literature, yet manual curation is inconsistently documented and large language model (LLM) extraction can hallucinate values and fabricate citations. We present MAPLE (Model-Aware Parameterization from Literature Evidence), which uses structured validation schemas as a collaboration interface between LLMs and modelers. Two schemas span two scales: the SubmodelTarget schema for isolated experiments constraining individual parameters, and the CalibrationTarget schema for clinical and in vivo endpoints constraining the full model. Both separate data extraction from modeling decisions, recording every value with full provenance. Targeted validators catch characteristic LLM errors by matching values to source snippets, resolving DOIs, and executing code. For a pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma QSP model, we used MAPLE to extract and curate 37 SubmodelTargets and 45 CalibrationTargets. Before any human review, the validators triggered 50 automated retries; every value carries a direct quote from its source and a verified citation; and 11 of 19 parameters are supported by more than one independent source.The LLM drafted usable forward models and code from context, while the modeler supplied the context and scientific judgment it cannot infer, revising forward-model choices in 65% of SubmodelTargets, priors in 46%, and source relevance in all files. This evaluation covers one model in one disease area, by a single group, so it characterizes the framework rather than establishing how broadly it generalizes. MAPLE records the modelers reasoning in a form that can be re-run and independently checked, so it is not lost when the modeling team changes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LLMs used for QSP model calibration can hallucinate values and citations. MAPLE uses structured validation schemas to separate data extraction from modeling decisions, catching characteristic LLM errors and ensuring provenance-rich, reliable calibration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在定量系统药理学（QSP）模型校准中，大语言模型提取数据时常产生幻觉值和伪造引用，而人工校对缺乏一致性文档。本文提出MAPLE框架，利用结构化验证模式作为人机协作接口，分离数据提取与建模决策，并通过定向验证器捕获典型的LLM提取错误，从而确保每个数据值的完整来源与可靠性，有效缓解特定领域应用中的大模型幻觉问题。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在定量系统药理学（QSP）模型校准中，大语言模型提取数据时常产生幻觉值和伪造引用，而人工校对缺乏一致性文档。本文提出MAPLE框架，利用结构化验证模式作为人机协作接口，分离数据提取与建模决策，并通过定向验证器捕获典型的LLM提取错误，从而确保每个数据值的完整来源与可靠性，有效缓解特定领域应用中的大模型幻觉问题。
